name: Magic Items
description: Rules for identifying, attuning to, and using magic items, including nine item categories with mechanics
introduction: Magic items are gleaned from the hoards of felled monsters or discovered in long-lost vaults. Such items grant capabilities a character could rarely have otherwise, or they complement their owner's capabilities in wondrous ways.

magic_item_rules.title: Magic Item Rules
magic_item_rules.overview: Rules for identifying, attuning to, and using magic items

attunement_prerequisites.title: Attunement Prerequisites
attunement_prerequisites.description: If a magic item has a class prerequisite, a creature must be a member of that class to attune to the item
attunement_prerequisites.spellcaster_requirement: If a creature must be a spellcaster to attune to an item, the creature qualifies if it can cast at least one spell using its traits or features, not by using a magic item or the like

items_made_for_specific_creatures.title: Items Made for Specific Creatures
items_made_for_specific_creatures.description: Magic items that are meant to be worn tend to magically adjust themselves to the wearer
items_made_for_specific_creatures.dm_discretion: You can decide that a magic item doesn't adjust its size to fit any wearer. For example, a particular armorer might make items usable only by folk who are sized and shaped like dwarves

unusual_anatomy.title: Unusual Anatomy
unusual_anatomy.description: Use your discretion to decide whether a creature can wear an item not made for its anatomy
examples: A ring placed on a tentacle might work
A yuan-ti with a snakelike tail instead of legs can't wear magic boots

paired_items.title: Paired Items
paired_items.description: You can allow exceptions to the rule that paired items must both be worn
paired_items.example: A character with only one arm might be able to use a single Glove of Missile Snaring so long as the matching glove is on their person

magic_item_categories.title: Magic Item Categories
magic_item_categories.overview: Every magic item belongs to a category. The nine categories provide different mechanics and usage rules

armor.name: Armor
armor.description: An item in the Armor category is typically a magical version of armor from Equipment
mechanics: Unless an armor's description notes otherwise, the armor must be worn for its magic to function
Some suits of magic armor specify the type of armor they are, such as Chain Mail or Plate Armor
If no type is specified, choose the type or determine it randomly

potions.name: Potions
potions.description: An item in the Potion category might be a magical brew that must be imbibed or an oil that must be applied to a creature or an object
potions.composition: A typical potion consists of 1 ounce of liquid in a vial

using_a_potion.title: Using a Potion
using_a_potion.action: Drinking a potion or administering it to another creature requires a Bonus Action
using_a_potion.oils: Applying an oil might take longer as specified in its description
using_a_potion.effect: Once used, a potion takes effect immediately, and it is used up

mixing_potions.title: Mixing Potions
mixing_potions.description: A character might drink one potion while still under the effects of another or pour several potions into a single container
mixing_potions.mechanics: The strange ingredients used in creating potions can result in unpredictable interactions
mixing_potions.procedure: When a character mixes two potions together, roll on the Potion Miscibility table
mixing_potions.multiple_potions: If more than two are combined, roll again for each subsequent potion, combining the results
mixing_potions.revelation: Unless the effects are immediately obvious, reveal them only when they become evident

rolls.result: Both potions lose their effects, and the mixture creates a magical explosion in a 5-foot-radius Sphere centered on itself. Each creature in that area takes 4d10 Force damage
Both potions lose their effects, and the mixture becomes an ingested poison of your choice (see Poison)
Both potions work, but with their numerical effects and durations halved. If a potion has no numerical effect and no duration, it instead loses its effect
Both potions work, but the numerical effects and duration of one potion are doubled. If neither potion has anything to double in this way, they work normally

Only one potion works, but its effects are permanent. Choose the simplest effect to make permanent or the one that seems the most fun. For example, a Potion of Healing might increase the drinker's Hit Point maximum by 2d4 + 2, or a Potion of Invisibility might give the drinker the Invisible condition indefinitely. At your discretion, a Dispel Magic spell or similar magic might end this lasting effect

rings.name: Rings
rings.description: For its magic to function, an item in the Ring category must be worn on a finger or a similar digit unless its description notes otherwise

rods.name: Rods
rods.description: An item in the Rod category is a scepter usually made of metal, wood, or bone
rods.characteristics: A typical rod weighs 2 to 5 pounds
rods.default_use: Unless its description notes otherwise, a rod can be used as an Arcane Focus

scrolls.name: Scrolls
scrolls.description: An item in the Scroll category is a roll of paper or parchment, sometimes attached to wooden rods and typically kept safe in a tube of ivory, jade, leather, metal, or wood
types: Spell Scroll (most prevalent) - a spell stored in written form
Other scrolls (like Scroll of Protection) - bear an incantation that isn't a spell

using_a_scroll.title: Using a Scroll
using_a_scroll.mechanism: Unleashing the magic in a scroll requires the user to read the scroll
using_a_scroll.consumption: When its magic has been invoked, the scroll can't be used again. Its words fade, or it crumbles into dust
using_a_scroll.accessibility: Any creature that can understand a written language can read a scroll and attempt to activate it unless its description notes otherwise

staffs.name: Staffs
staffs.description: Items in the Staff category vary widely in appearance
appearance_variations: Some are of nearly equal diameter throughout and smooth
Some are made of wood, others of polished metal or crystal
staffs.characteristics: A staff weighs between 2 and 7 pounds and serves well as a walking stick or cane
default_uses: Unless its description notes otherwise, a staff can be used as a nonmagical Quarterstaff
Unless its description notes otherwise, a staff can be used as an Arcane Focus

wands.name: Wands
wands.description: An item in the Wand category is typically 12 to 15 inches long and crafted of metal, bone, or wood
wands.construction: It is tipped with metal, crystal, stone, or some other material
wands.default_use: Unless its description notes otherwise, a wand can be used as an Arcane Focus

weapons.name: Weapons
weapons.description: A magic weapon is typically a magical version of a weapon from Equipment
weapons.type_specification: Some magic weapons specify the type of weapon they are in their descriptions, such as a Longsword or Longbow
weapons.unspecified_weapons: If no weapon type is specified, you may choose the type or determine it randomly

ammunition.title: Ammunition
ammunition.mechanics: If a magic weapon has the Ammunition property, ammunition fired from it is considered magical for the purpose of any rule that cares whether a weapon is magical or not

wondrous_items.name: Wondrous Items
examples: Wearable items such as boots, belts, capes, amulets, brooches, and circlets
Bags, carpets, figurines, horns, musical instruments, and more
wondrous_items.description: Wondrous Items include a diverse range of magical equipment that doesn't fit other categories

contains: Magic item rules and attunement prerequisites
Items made for specific creatures and unusual anatomy rules
Nine magic item categories (Armor, Potions, Rings, Rods, Scrolls, Staffs, Wands, Weapons, Wondrous Items)
Armor category rules and type selection
Potion mixing mechanics (Potion Miscibility table with 8 outcomes)
Rod composition and Arcane Focus use
Staff appearance variations and dual uses (weapon + focus)
Wand construction and Arcane Focus use
Weapon type specification and magical ammunition
